page: Amazon Web Services c5a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, falls in the bottom 10%, representing a potential bottleneck for single-threaded tasks. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks consistently rank in the middle 50%. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in specific areas, with Passmark CPU encryption and memory scores placing in the top 25%. Additionally, small-model LLM inference tasks achieve strong results, processing 2204.68 tokens per second for prompts and 250.66 tokens per second for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5a.12xlarge leverages AMD EPYC processors to deliver a high-density compute configuration at a lower cost profile compared to Intel-based equivalents. The primary tradeoffs include the absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage, and the lower single-core execution speed. This server is appropriate for multi-threaded workloads such as batch processing, software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor, it delivers 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) at 3.3 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It features no local storage and no GPU, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core CPU benchmarks place in the bottom 10%, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth reside in the middle 50%. Notably, Passmark memory and CPU encryption tests, along with small-model LLM inference, rank in the top 25%. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective compute-dense option for multi-threaded workloads, including software compilation, static web serving, and batch processing, though it is constrained by lower single-threaded execution speeds and a lack of local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor, delivering balanced multi-core performance and strong encryption capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-dense, x86_64 server featuring forty-eight virtual cores and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores at 3.3 GHz
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Strong performance in CPU encryption and memory benchmarks
Baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps with Nitro hypervisor integration
Best suited for multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, and software compilation
